opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: Running agents at the OS level introduces significant IPC complexity and security surface area. You'll likely find that context persistence across application boundaries costs more latency than you'd expect, often negating the perceived "seamlessness." Consider whether your agents truly need OS-level privileges or if a properly instrumented sandboxed layer provides better isolation without the integration debt.
actions.reasoning: I have hands-on experience building multi-agent systems with tool orchestration and OS-level integration patterns for AI agents.
